THE ENDS OF JUSTICE ARE PROMOTED BY TRANSFERRING THE CONTRA COSTA ACTION (CASE MSC13-00783) TO THIS COURT AND CONSOLIDATING IT WITH THIS ACTION. THE ACTIONS ARE NOT COMPLEX. COMMON QUESTIONS OF FACT AND LAW PREDOMINATE AND ARE SIGNIFICANT TO THE LITIGATION. DTG DEFENDANTS AGREE THAT TRANSFER AND CONSOLIDATE WILL PROMOTE THE ENDS OF JUSTICE CONSISTENT WITH THE STANDARDS SET FORTH IN CCP 404.1. THE ONLY DISPUTED ISSUE IS WHERE THE MATTER SHOULD BE TRANSFERRED. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFFS IN THIS CASE AND IN THE CONTRA COSTA ACTION STIPULATE THAT THE CONTRA COSTA ACTION SHOULD BE TRANSFERRED TO SAN FRANCISCO. THE CONVENIENCE OF THE PARTIES IS PROMOTED IN SAN FRANCISCO. DEFENDANT DTG MAINTAINS OFFICES AND CONDUCTS BUSINESS IN SAN FRANCISCO, ONE OF ITS EMPLOYEE DEFENDANTS RESIDES HERE, AND ANOTHER IN SAN MATEO COUNTY. IT IS NOT INCONVENIENT FOR DEFENSE COUNSEL, WITH OFFICES IN OAKLAND, TO COME TO SAN FRANCISCO FOR PROCEEDINGS IN THIS CASE. AS FOR WITNESSES, SOME ARE IN SAN FRANCISCO AND OTHERS ARE IN THE EAST BAY, BUT THERE IS NO SIGNIFICANT REASON TO TRANSFER THE CASE TO CONTRA COSTA ON THAT ACCOUNT GIVEN THE PROXIMITY OF THE TWO COUNTIES. FURTHERMORE, COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF IN THE CONTRA COSTA ACTION (CITY OF PINOLE) HAS STIPULATED TO HAVING ITS CASE HEARD IN SAN FRANCISCO, WHICH IS A STRONG INDICATION THAT WITNESSES IN THAT CASE WILL NOT BE INCONVENIENCED BY THE PROPOSED TRANSFER WHICH, TAKING INTO ACCOUNT ALL FACTORS, PROMOTES THE INTERESTS OF JUSTICE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

Off Calendar Jul-22-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS DANA JOHNSON, JOSEPH SKELTON and DTG OPERATIONS, INC.'S Motion For Leave To File An Amended Answer is GRANTED. Amendments to pleadings are to be liberally granted. No prejudice exists here, where discovery has already been taken on the relevant issues. The court's prior denial of defendants' motion for summary judgment does not eliminate the possibility of a superseding cause affirmative defense. The court's order did not articulate its grounds for denying the motion; defendants may have failed to meet their burden of establishing lack of duty, or plaintiff may have raised disputed issues of fact. In any event, the affirmative defense has not been finally adjudicated. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Ntc Of Joinder In Timothy Cauwels And Leslie Cauwels Mtn For An Order To Trnasfer And Consolidate
Off Calendar Jul-30-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MICHAEL GATTO, ESQ., AND JOSEPH LUCIA, ESQ., 925.609.1699, FOR PLAINTIFFS TIMOTHY CAUWELS AND LESLIE CAUWELS.
MATTHEW ROMAN, ESQ., 510.287.4170, FOR DEFENDANT DTG OPERATIONS, INC. DBA DOLLAR RENT A CAR.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF CITY OF PINOLE POLICE DEPARTMENT, PSI ADJUSTED BY MUNICIPAL POOLING AUTHORITY Ntc Of Joinder In Timothy Cauwels And Leslie Cauwels Mtn For An Order To Trnasfer And Consolidate IS Granted. The ends of justice are promoted by transferring the Contra Costa action (Case MSC13-00783) to this Court and consolidating it with this action. The actions are not complex. Common questions of fact and law predominate and are significant to the litigation. DTG Defendants agree that transfer and consolidate will promote the ends of justice consistent with the standards set forth in CCP 404.1. The only disputed issue is where the matter should be transferred. Counsel for plaintiffs in this case and in the Contra Costa action stipulate that the Contra Costa action should be transferred to San Francisco. The convenience of the parties is promoted in San Francisco. Defendant DTG maintains offices and conducts business in San Francisco, one of its employee defendants resides here, and another in San Mateo County. It is not inconvenient for defense counsel, with offices in Oakland, to come to San Francisco for proceedings in this case. As for witnesses, some are in San Francisco and others are in the East Bay, but there is no significant reason to transfer the case to Contra Costa on that account given the proximity of the two counties. Furthermore, counsel for Plaintiff in the Contra Costa action (City of Pinole) has stipulated to having its case heard in San Francisco, which is a strong indication that witnesses in that case will not be inconvenienced by the proposed transfer which, taking into account all factors, promotes the interests of justice. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
